stories.headline: Iran War: US Seize Iranian Ship & Peace Talks Uncertain | Daybreak Europe 4/20/2026
stories.summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. 
                                                                                                                                                                                                      Tensions in the Iran war ratcheted up over the weekend as th

stories.headline: U.S. Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship Touska in Arabian Sea
stories.summary: The military said it had disabled the vessel after it ignored repeated warnings to stop, amid a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. Marines boarded the ship and seized it.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran will skip talks as tensions rise over US ship seizure
stories.summary: Attack comes hours after US President Donald Trump announced he is sending his team to Islamabad for possible talks.

stories.headline: Tehran vows swift response after US seizes Iranian-flagged vessel near the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US said its naval blockade of Iranian ports will continue despite the ongoing ceasefire, prompting Iran to U-turn on its reopening of the Strait of Hormuz just hours after it declared it open for maritime traffic, and firing at vessels trying to transit.

stories.headline: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills at least three people in the Caribbean Sea
stories.summary: US strikes on drug boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean have so far killed a minimum of 181 people. Critics have slammed the Trump administration over the strikes, saying they constitute a violation of international law.

stories.headline: US releases video said to show mission to capture Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.headline: Oil rebounds as Iran threatens to retaliate for US attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Hopes for peace deal fade amid uncertainty over possible talks in Pakistan this week

stories.headline: Has the U.S. Seizure of an Iranian Ship Doomed Peace Talks? | Insight with Haslinda Amin 04/20/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.headline: India Widens Russian Insurance List as Iran War Hurts Oil Flows
stories.summary: India allowed more Russian insurers to provide cover for vessels calling at its ports, while extending permits for others, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz hits energy shipments from the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: US military releases video of marines seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: Video from the US military show an operation by its forces to seize an Iranian-flagged ship near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Moment US navy fires on and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
stories.summary: US military footage shows a destroyer firing warning shots before boarding and seizing an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it tried to evade a naval blockade, amid heightened tensions near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: As barbed wire blocks kids from class, Palestinians stage ‘Freedom School’
stories.summary: Children from Umm al-Khair in the occupied West Bank protest after setters erect a fence blocking access to school.

stories.headline: Rumen Radev wins Bulgarian Parliamentary election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in the Bulgarian parliament.

stories.headline: FTSE bosses receive 18% pay bump this year in global fight for talent
stories.summary: Companies are also watering down ESG elements of pay plans as boards reassess whether such awards ‘make sense’

stories.headline: Russian economy is faltering despite oil windfall, Sweden warns
stories.summary: Stockholm’s military intelligence head says Moscow is manipulating data to make its economy look better

stories.headline: Hormuz Standoff Keeps Markets on Edge | The Asia Trade 4/20/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Avril Hong, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
stories.summary: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

stories.headline: Daily Mail’s ‘aggressive’ reporting on Prince Harry and Meghan ‘irreparably damaged’ press briefings

stories.headline: Pro-Russian Rumen Radev on course for runaway victory in Bulgarian elections
stories.summary: Stronger-than-expected win for former president could end years of weak coalition governmentsThe pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev is on course for a runaway victory in Bulgaria’s election, official results show, potentially ending years of weak coalition governments and sidelining long-dominant political forces.The performance, surpassing opinion polls, is one of the strongest results by a single party in a generation and may end, for now

stories.headline: Hormuz crisis delivers a Taiwan Strait wake-up call: 5 things to know
Oil prices climb as US-Iran stand-off keeps Strait of Hormuz in limbo
stories.summary: Oil prices climbed more than 5% while European markets fell on Monday morning as a stand-off between Iran and the US prevented tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.headline: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries have responded to rising oil prices

stories.headline: Odd Lots: The Economics of American Oil Production (Podcast)
Saxo Sees 'Confusion' Reigning in Oil Markets
stories.summary: Ole Hansen, commodity strategy head at Saxo Bank, discusses market sentiment as oil jumped amid a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions. "Even with the setback we saw on Friday, these prices are still too high relative to what potential consumers are prepared to pay," Hansen tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Revolut CEO Storonsky Says Digital Bank’s IPO Is Two Years Out
stories.summary: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky said he wants to take the London-based digital bank public, but not until at least 2028, extending the timeline on one of Europe’s most anticipated offerings.

stories.headline: Zimbabwe’s Currency Is Undervalued by Nearly Half, Governor Says
stories.summary: Zimbabwe’s currency is undervalued by almost half, the governor of the central bank said, pointing to the foreign reserves and gold that back the unit.

Trump says US seized Iranian ship trying to get past blockade near Hormuz

stories.headline: Oil and Gas Jump After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Imperils Talks
stories.summary: Oil and natural gas prices soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: US forces attack and seize Iranian ship Touska near Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.

stories.headline: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.summary: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.headline: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Two police officers suspended after fleeing Kyiv shooting scene
stories.summary: An inquiry was opened after a video showed officers fleeing in panic from Saturday’s shooting, which left six dead and many injured. The patrol police chief has resigned.

stories.headline: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: Stocks, Bonds Fall as Oil Jumps After Hormuz Chaos: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil jumped, pushing US stocks and Treasuries lower after a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on the prospects for peace talks ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
stories.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

stories.headline: Michigan Gas Clerk Helps Save Kidnapped Teen Girl Who Mouthed ‘Help’
stories.summary: A fellow student who had witnessed the kidnapping called the police, and other students helped track the girl to a gas station.

stories.headline: Democrats Eye a Broader Battlefield to Capture Congress in November
stories.summary: Weighed down by President Trump’s approval ratings, some Republican incumbents are struggling to raise money while Democrats look for targets like a Tennessee seat south of Nashville.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan
stories.summary: The Department of Justice is seeking ballots and other materials from the 2024 election. Michigan officials call it election interference.

stories.headline: Muslim Southerners Face a Fresh Wave of Hateful Political Rhetoric
stories.summary: Some Muslim voters were once drawn to Republican positions on family values and individual liberty, but as Southern politicians stoke anti-Islamic sentiment, many feel threatened.

stories.headline: Potential 2028 Democrats Audition in Michigan,With a Focus on Trump
stories.summary: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.

stories.headline: Who will shape the global agenda, the left or far right?
stories.summary: Left-wing leaders meet in Barcelona, while far-right protesters march in Milan.

stories.headline: Bulgaria election: Ex-President Radev claims ‘victory’ after early results
stories.summary: Ex-PM Boyko Borissov congratulates Radev, cautioning that 'winning elections is one thing, governing is another'.

stories.headline: Israeli ministers celebrate re-establishment of Sa-Nur West Bank settlement
stories.summary: Sa-Nur is one of four former West Bank settlements approved by the government two decades after settlers were evicted.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
stories.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.headline: Why China is taking a behind-the-scenes role in the Iran war
stories.summary: As Trump pursued an unpopular war, Beijing positioned itself as a peacemaker, with little incentive to pressure Iran or annoy the U.S.

stories.headline: As sexual misconduct allegations rock Congress, some push for new rules
stories.summary: The resignations of two lawmakers facing ethics investigations have drawn comparisons to the #MeToo movement. Some House members say it’s time to revisit their rules and culture.

stories.headline: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.headline: He made jazz under air raids - and built an Indian city's music scene
stories.summary: War correspondent, jazz bandleader and impresario, KC Sen shaped Kolkata’s music scene.

stories.headline: Chinese carmaker patents voice-controlled 'in-vehicle toilet'
stories.summary: Seres' plans show how stiff competition in the EV space is putting pressure on carmakers to innovate.

stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
